mechanism_narrative: When real activity is supported by rising retail sales, firms receive near-term revenue confirmation and equity earnings expectations are revised upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 2.5 percent as of February 2005
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll growth and falling unemployment signal broad income gains without labor-market deterioration, supporting consumption and earnings, and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 132843.0 and UNRATE was 5.4 as of February 2005
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up unemployment-down condition has no higher 60-day forward return than either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising housing starts indicate strong household formation, construction demand, and credit availability, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 2000 and is above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST above 2000 and above its 6-month average is followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with a rising policy rate signals inflation pressure that forces tighter monetary policy, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 191.3 as of January 2005 and FEDFUNDS was 2.5 as of February 2005
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-acceleration and rate-rise interaction does not predict lower 20-day returns than CPIAUCSL acceleration alone or FEDFUNDS increases alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger goods-sector output and operating leverage, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 forward returns or a Rank IC below 0.03 in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening income, lower consumption, and rising recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 5.4 percent as of February 2005
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases above the trigger are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales alongside rising payroll employment confirms that final demand is supported by labor income, improving revenue breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 352138.0 and PAYEMS was 132843.0 as of February 2005
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up PAYEMS-up state fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 basis points on average in walk-forward testing.
